items.label: Movies - AI Animation
items.excerpt: Annecy's MIFA animation market is centering AI tools and private investment as the defining forces reshaping animation financing in 2026, hosting closed-door workshops where studios debate how generative AI changes the economics of character, environment, and background production across feature and episodic pipelines.

items.label: Movies - AI VFX and Post-Production
items.excerpt: Adobe has agreed to acquire Topaz Labs — the Emmy-winning AI company behind Video AI's upscaling, denoising, and motion interpolation — with plans to integrate its on-device NeuroStream AI models into Firefly and Creative Cloud, expanding the toolkit from AI generation to AI restoration for filmmakers in a single ecosystem.

items.label: Movies - AI Animation
items.excerpt: SIGGRAPH 2026's Computer Animation Festival has been announced with a Google DeepMind production session on artist-driven AI-assisted expressionistic animation and a Best in Show for 'Apart,' a 4-technique animated short — previewing the AI-human workflow debates that will define the July conference in Los Angeles.

items.label: Movies - Hollywood AI Industry
items.excerpt: Deadline's investigation reveals that a major AI technology company is used across a large share of Hollywood productions but credited on only about one-fifth of them — drawing a parallel to cosmetic surgery's unspoken culture and raising urgent transparency questions as studios quietly deploy AI for VFX cleanup, dialogue polish, and color grading.

items.label: Movies - Hollywood AI Industry
items.excerpt: IndieWire analyzes the fierce fan backlash to A24's $75 million Google DeepMind partnership — examining the studio's argument that co-designing AI tools with DeepMind gives filmmakers a voice in shaping technology, versus an audience that equates any AI adjacency with a betrayal of A24's artistic brand promise.

items.label: Movies - AI Animation
items.excerpt: IndieWire's rolling Annecy 2026 roundup captures the festival's AI undercurrent alongside new project announcements, with Autodesk Flow Studio demoing live-action-to-3D-scene conversion on the show floor and studios at MIFA negotiating how generative tools reshape animation co-production deals, indie finance models, and the creative economy.

items.label: Movies - AI VFX and Post-Production
items.excerpt: postPerspective examines Adobe's Topaz Labs acquisition from a post-production workflow angle, noting how on-device NeuroStream AI for video upscaling, denoising, and motion interpolation integrates into Premiere and Firefly without cloud dependency — a meaningful change for offline edit suites and secure post facilities.

items.label: Movies - AI Animation
items.excerpt: Deadline's five Annecy 2026 talking points places AI at the center of the market's agenda — with MIFA running closed-door AI workflow workshops and studios debating how generative tools are disrupting animation production costs, IP protection strategies, and co-production norms before industry standards can catch up.

items.label: Movies - AI VFX and Post-Production
items.excerpt: RedShark News rounds up Adobe's June 2026 Creative Cloud update for video professionals: rebuilt AI rotoscoping in Premiere (Object Matte with four AI selection tools replacing Roto Brush), Reflection Removal for glass-shot cleanup, and an offline-capable on-device Remove Tool — practical AI upgrades to everyday post-production workflows.

items.label: Movies - Hollywood AI Industry
items.excerpt: Jurassic World Rebirth and Rogue One director Gareth Edwards calls AI 'bigger than CGI' — describing diffusion model tools as a 'second-unit director who is a billionaire on acid' — and argues the technology's value lies in rapid creative iteration during concepting, not in replacing directors' final production judgment.
